{"step_by_step_thinking": "Liraglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist approved for weight loss in adults with a BMI ≥30 kg/m² or ≥27 kg/m² with at least one weight-related comorbidity. Multiple sources confirm its efficacy for weight reduction. Document [0] states that liraglutide is effective for weight management in patients with obesity. Document [2] specifies approximate weight loss of 4% to 5.4% of total body weight. Document [12] notes that randomized controlled trials have demonstrated liraglutide's efficacy in weight loss. Document [19] and [23] compare it to other agents, showing that liraglutide has demonstrated sustained weight loss over time. Even though newer agents like semaglutide and tirzepatide show higher efficacy, liraglutide still provides clinically meaningful weight loss, with 63.2% of participants in the SCALE trial achieving at least 5% weight loss. The UMLS documents confirm that liraglutide is a member of the GLP-1 analogue class and that its pharmacologic actions include appetite suppression and satiety, which contribute to weight reduction. Therefore, despite being less effective than newer agents, liraglutide is clearly effective for weight reduction.", "answer_choice": "A"}
